technical: Patient(?p) ^ hasERStatus(?p, Positive) ^ hasPRStatus(?p, Positive) ^ hasHER2Status(?p, Negative) ^ hasKi67(?p, ?k) ^ swrlb:lessThan(?k, 20) -> hasMolecularSubtype(?p, LuminalA)
natural: If ER and PR are positive and HER2 is negative and Ki-67 < 20%, classify as Luminal A subtype

technical: Patient(?p) ^ hasERStatus(?p, Positive) ^ hasPRStatus(?p, Positive) ^ hasHER2Status(?p, Negative) ^ hasKi67(?p, ?k) ^ swrlb:greaterThanOrEqual(?k, 20) -> hasMolecularSubtype(?p, LuminalB)
natural: If ER and PR are positive and HER2 is negative and Ki-67 ≥ 20%, classify as Luminal B subtype
If HER2 is positive, classify as HER2-positive subtype

technical: Patient(?p) ^ hasERStatus(?p, Negative) ^ hasPRStatus(?p, Negative) ^ hasHER2Status(?p, Negative) -> hasMolecularSubtype(?p, TripleNegative)
natural: If ER, PR, and HER2 are all negative, classify as Triple Negative subtype
If tumor size > 5cm, classify as high risk

technical: Patient(?p) ^ hasLymphNodeStatus(?p, Positive) ^ hasPositiveNodes(?p, ?n) ^ swrlb:greaterThan(?n, 3) -> hasRiskLevel(?p, High)
natural: If lymph node positive with >3 positive nodes, classify as high risk
If tumor grade is 3, classify as high risk
If lymphovascular invasion present, classify as intermediate risk
If Luminal A subtype, recommend endocrine therapy
If Luminal B subtype, recommend chemotherapy plus endocrine therapy
If HER2-positive subtype, recommend anti-HER2 therapy
If Triple Negative subtype, recommend chemotherapy

technical: Patient(?p) ^ hasImagingStudy(?p, ?study) ^ hasTextureScore(?study, ?t) ^ swrlb:greaterThan(?t, 0.8) ^ hasShapeIrregularity(?study, ?s) ^ swrlb:greaterThan(?s, 0.75) -> hasRiskLevel(?p, High)
natural: If imaging shows texture > 0.8 AND shape irregularity > 0.75, classify as high risk

technical: Patient(?p) ^ hasImagingStudy(?p, ?study) ^ hasEnhancementScore(?study, ?e) ^ swrlb:greaterThan(?e, 0.9) -> hasSuspicionLevel(?p, High)
natural: If enhancement score > 0.9, classify as high suspicion
If high risk, require 3-month follow-up interval
If intermediate risk, require 6-month follow-up interval
If low risk, require 12-month follow-up interval
If BRCA positive, recommend genetic counseling
If strong family history, recommend genetic testing
If treatment response < 50%, require treatment adjustment
If pathological complete response, classify as favorable prognosis

technical: Patient(?p) ^ hasDataQualityScore(?p, ?q) ^ swrlb:greaterThan(?q, 90) ^ hasConsentStatus(?p, Approved) ^ hasImagingQuality(?p, Standard) -> includeInFederatedLearning(?p, true)
natural: Include in federated learning if: data quality > 90%, consent approved, imaging meets standards
